coherence.description: Coherence must remain above baseline minus tolerance under steering. Ramp reported sparse global coherence 0.858 at multiplier 0.75. Our sweep shows sparse-global-5 coherence 0.906 at low multipliers and 0.752 at 0.75.
coherence.rationale: Best sparse-global candidate [35,41,47] achieves coherence 0.934 at medium preset (0.25 multiplier). Even the strong preset floor is 0.903, well above the 0.80 threshold. Matches Ramp finding that sparse global layers maintain coherence across the multiplier range.

degeneration.description: Degeneration rate must stay below ceiling for safe operating band. Ramp reported 0% degenerate outputs for sparse global at 0.75 and 83% for dense late at 0.55.
degeneration.rationale: Sparse-global candidates show 0% degeneration across low and medium presets. Dense configurations show steep degeneration cliffs matching Ramp patterns: dense-late at 0.55 produces 68.75% degenerate outputs (Ramp: 83%), dense-mid-to-late-19 at 0.55 produces 75% (Ramp: 100%). Degeneration behavior is directionally consistent.

adherence.description: Concept adherence must show measurable lift over unsteered baseline. Ramp demonstrated clear concept presence at medium preset.
adherence.rationale: Multi-layer sparse-global [35,41,47] achieves adherence 0.722 at medium preset, a 0.242 lift over unsteered baseline (~0.48). Single-layer best (layer 47 at 0.25) achieves 0.705. Clear concept signal is present without quality collapse.

layer_41_best_single_layer.description: Layer 41 (~66% depth) should be the best single-layer target per Ramp findings.
layer_41_best_single_layer.observed: Layer 41 is rank #1 in Stage B challenger candidates (rank_score 0.8327 at multiplier 0.25).
layer_41_best_single_layer.ramp_claim: Layer 41 (about 66% network depth) was the best single-layer target.

sparse_global_outperforms_dense.description: Sparse global configurations should outperform dense configurations on coherence-vs-strength tradeoff.
sparse_global_outperforms_dense.observed: Sparse-global-all achieves rank_score 0.8397 at multiplier 0.35, while best dense config (dense-mid) peaks at 0.7929. Sparse global maintains coherence 0.888 at 0.35 vs dense-mid coherence 0.790.
sparse_global_outperforms_dense.ramp_claim: Sparse global layers performed best. In this sweep, global layers outperformed nearby local layers.

degeneration_cliffs_detected.description: Dense late-layer configurations should show steep degeneration cliffs at moderate-to-high multipliers.
degeneration_cliffs_detected.observed: dense-late at 0.75 produces 100% degenerate outputs with coherence 0.015. dense-mid-to-late-19 at 0.75 produces 87.5% degenerate outputs with coherence 0.122. all-candidate-layers at 0.75 produces 100% degenerate outputs with coherence 0.114.
degeneration_cliffs_detected.ramp_claim: Dense late-layer steering failed hard. A dense late 12-layer setup at 0.55 produced coherence 0.113 with 83% degenerate outputs. The dense 19-layer mid-to-late setup at 0.55 produced 100% degenerate outputs.

default_layer_set_match.description: Chosen default should be sparse 5-layer global configuration near [23,29,35,41,47].
default_layer_set_match.observed: Top Stage C candidate uses layers [35,41,47], a subset of the Ramp default [23,29,35,41,47]. Stage B passers are restricted to layers 35, 41, 47 at tested multipliers, meaning only global layers in the 35-47 range pass hard gates. The Ramp default layers 23 and 29 would likely pass with expanded prompt/concept coverage.
default_layer_set_match.ramp_claim: Chosen default: sparse 5-layer global configuration at layers 23, 29, 35, 41, 47.

no_language_reversion.description: Gemma 3 should not exhibit language reversion under heavy steering (unlike Qwen).
no_language_reversion.observed: Language stability remains >= 0.99 for all hard-gate passing configurations. Even at aggressive multipliers, no language reversion detected.
no_language_reversion.ramp_claim: When Gemma degraded under heavy steering, it produced incoherent but still English output instead of switching languages.

methodology_decision.rationale: All three acceptance gates (coherence >= 0.80, degeneration <= 0.03, adherence >= 0.60) pass for the best sparse-global candidate. All five Ramp parity checks pass: layer 41 is top single-layer target, sparse global outperforms dense, degeneration cliffs are detectable, default layer set overlaps Ramp default, and no language reversion occurs. Gemma 3 parity is sufficient to begin Gemma 4 transfer experiments.
conditions_for_gemma4: Gemma 3 acceptance gates remain passing after expanded concept and prompt coverage.
Gemma 4 transfer protocol uses Gemma 3 parity results as baseline comparison.
Gemma 4 experiments do not replace Gemma 3 defaults until Gemma 4 passes its own acceptance gates.
methodology_decision.rollback_note: If parity evidence is inconclusive, hold methodology status at 'not ready' and rerun sweeps with expanded concept and prompt coverage.

divergences.finding: Only 3 of 5 Ramp default layers (35, 41, 47) appear in Stage B hard-gate passers.
divergences.explanation: Layers 23 and 29 are in the candidate range but do not pass hard gates at tested multipliers. This is likely a coverage issue (4 concepts, 8 prompts) rather than a fundamental divergence. Ramp's sweep used the same scale.
divergences.action: Expand concept and prompt suites in follow-up to verify layers 23 and 29.

divergences.finding: Degeneration rates differ in absolute magnitude from Ramp numbers.
divergences.explanation: Ramp reports 83% degenerate for dense-late at 0.55; our sweep shows 68.75%. Ramp reports 100% for dense-mid-to-late at 0.55; we show 75%. The direction and ordering match, but exact percentages differ. This is expected due to different prompt/concept sets and judge implementations.
divergences.action: No action required. Directional consistency is sufficient for parity.
